insight: Japan's performance during hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup has been notable, as they managed to gain momentum in both of their breaks, with no significant change in their pace. This suggests that the breaks did not disrupt their rhythm, and they were able to maintain their intensity after the brief pauses. When Japan was pressing before a hydration break, they were able to keep their momentum going afterwards, which is a positive sign that they can sustain their aggressive play even after a stoppage. Overall, the hydration breaks seem to have had a neutral to slightly positive effect on Japan's performance, allowing them to regroup and continue playing at their previous level without losing steam.
